steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The authors claim all people naturally have an emotional attachment to where they live. The correct quotation must address home and its universal emotional power.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Home is home, to the lowly as well as the great; and no rank, or color destroys its sacred character, its power over the mind, and the affections.": This directly supports the claim. ✓
  • Choice B — "Let us, dear reader, remember the punishment of idle curiosity, as taught in the true and affecting history [named] 'Blue Beard;' and, striving to be content with the facts in the case, seek not to lift the veil, which the sensibility of true love, and feminine delicacy, have alike conspired to draw.": a narrative detail unrelated to home's emotional power. ✗
  • Choice C — "There is often a kind of [deceptive] light, playing around such [famous] names, calculated to dazzle and mislead, by their false lustre, until the eye can no longer receive the pure light of Truth, or the mind appreciate real excellence, or intrinsic worth.": about fame, not home or attachment. ✗
  • Choice D — "Blessed are the slumbers of the innocent! They are kindlier than balm, and they refresh and gladden the spirit of childhood, like ministerings from a better world.": addresses sleep, not home. ✗
That matches Choice A — "Home is home, to the lowly as well as the great; and no rank, or color destroys its sacred character, its power over the mind, and the affections.".
